activation_classifications.name: Initial
activation_classifications.definition: Required for the first production implementation.

activation_classifications.name: First vertical slice
activation_classifications.definition: Required specifically for the approved first vertical slice.

activation_classifications.name: Portability gate
activation_classifications.definition: Required to demonstrate portability through the approved second-Workspace validation boundary.

activation_classifications.name: Conditional launch capability
activation_classifications.definition: Included only when its approved qualification conditions are met.

activation_classifications.name: Post-initial deferred
activation_classifications.definition: Retained as governed architecture but not built in the initial implementation.

activation_classifications.name: Reassessment-boundary capability
activation_classifications.definition: Considered when scale, security, cost, or operating evidence crosses an approved threshold.

in_scope.statement: Canonical documentation sources and publication tooling
First governed execution slice: one process, one database, one Domain Package, and a no-op command adapter while exercising the full governing chain
Initial connector and MCP proof: live and fixture-based read-only acquisition, governed query and refresh, Review Service access, and no-op Command/Outcome loop
First governed AI slice using one provider, one narrow task, accepted evidence, structured output, and Review Service handoff
First Operations Page limited to review, refresh, workflow inspection, data quality, strategy review, and Command/Outcome status
Initial production security posture: allowlisted enrollment, external OIDC, server-side sessions, typed authorization, RLS, encryption, audit, and security readiness gate
Layered testing, provider-neutral observability, reliability, reconciliation, operational conformance, and production runbooks
Single-process production, immutable release identity, rehearsed recovery, governed activation, and formal production readiness
Initial vertical slice, measurable build gates, read-only first-domain acquisition, governed review/action, and authoritative fallback interfaces
Modular monorepo, package boundaries, inward dependency direction, authoritative contracts, minimal local/test foundation, and governed first slice
Python 3.14 and uv baseline, strict typing, CLI-first interaction, PostgreSQL persistence, provider-neutral model boundary, and portable development
Single PostgreSQL transactional foundation, immutable artifacts, hybrid payloads, durable workflows, replay-safe propagation, relational Memory/Knowledge, and reviewed migrations
Compiled authority graph, strict configuration schemas, governed activation, environment narrowing, typed secret references, and explicit Private Strategy scope

out_of_scope.statement: DIP product and runtime implementation coding
Initial dependency on draft MCP features or custom transports
Local password database and public self-registration
Remote production exposure before the security readiness gate
Numeric service objectives before deployment evidence
Initial Kubernetes, Redis, brokers, vector databases, or separate workers
SQLite as a PostgreSQL behavioral-test substitute
Foundational dependency on a third-party agent framework
Unrestricted recursive configuration merge, network includes, executable templates, or arbitrary authority-root scanning

deferrals.title: DIP product/runtime implementation
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: All product and runtime implementation work is deferred until IKG0 is formally granted.

deferrals.title: Separate command/query infrastructure
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Separate command and query infrastructure is deferred; the initial architecture uses CQRS-lite within the shared application and persistence foundation.

deferrals.title: Out-of-process and distributed workflow execution
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Distributed execution is deferred; the initial runner is in-process and sequential behind durable dispatcher and runner interfaces.

deferrals.title: Dynamic Domain Package registration
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Dynamic package discovery is deferred; initial capability registration is explicit, allowlisted, and static at the composition root.

deferrals.title: Webhook-based acquisition
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Webhook delivery remains a future acquisition mode; initial acquisition uses scheduled and manual pull through the Receipt pipeline.

deferrals.title: Distributed rate limiting
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Distributed rate limiting is deferred; the initial limiter is process-local with durable source cooldown metadata.

deferrals.title: Draft MCP features and custom transports
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Draft MCP features and custom transports are deferred until stable compatibility evidence justifies adoption.

deferrals.title: Vector retrieval
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Vector retrieval is deferred until demonstrated retrieval need justifies it; initial retrieval uses PostgreSQL relational, lineage, graph, and metadata search.

deferrals.title: Autonomous AI tool loop
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: An autonomous model tool loop is deferred; initial AI tools are read-only and the first slice requires no autonomous loop.

deferrals.title: Multi-provider production routing
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Multi-provider production routing is deferred; initial routing is deterministic and the first AI slice uses one provider behind the provider-neutral gateway.

deferrals.title: Single-page-application framework
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: A SPA framework is deferred; the Operations Page is server-rendered and progressively enhanced.

deferrals.title: Realtime UI transport
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Realtime UI transport is deferred; initial interface updates use reloads or polling.

deferrals.title: External policy-engine product
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: An external policy-engine product is deferred; initial authorization uses typed hybrid role- and attribute-based policy within DIP.

deferrals.title: Broader and public enrollment
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Broader enrollment and public self-registration are deferred; initial production enrollment is allowlisted and single-owner operationally.

deferrals.title: Remote production exposure
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Remote production exposure is deferred until the security readiness gate passes.

deferrals.title: Numeric service objectives
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Numeric service objectives are deferred until deployment evidence establishes meaningful baselines.

deferrals.title: Multi-region and high-availability topology
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Multi-region and high-availability production topology are deferred; initial production is single-region and non-HA.

deferrals.title: Additional distributed infrastructure
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Kubernetes, Redis, brokers, vector databases, and separate workers are deferred from the initial topology.

deferrals.title: Always-on staging
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: An always-on staging environment is deferred; local, CI, and production remain isolated.

deferrals.title: ChatGPT/MCP launch activation
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: ChatGPT/MCP launch activation is conditional on the compatibility spike; the authenticated API and Operations Page remain the fallback.

deferrals.title: Market-valued recommendations
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Market-valued recommendations are deferred until the market-intelligence qualification gate passes.

deferrals.title: External first-domain mutation
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: External first-domain mutation is deferred; the initial release is read-only and demonstrates an internal or no-op Command/Outcome path.

deferrals.title: Calendar-locked implementation schedule
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Calendar-locked scheduling is deferred until completed-work evidence supports reliable estimation.

deferrals.title: Unmeasured infrastructure expansion
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Additional infrastructure is deferred until measured need or a new ADR justifies it.

deferrals.title: Governed cross-Domain-Package composition
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Cross-Domain-Package composition is deferred until a governed mechanism replaces direct imports.

deferrals.title: Browser-facing TypeScript runtime
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: TypeScript and Node.js are deferred until the Operations Page or another browser-facing interface begins.

deferrals.title: FastAPI installation and API slice
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: FastAPI installation is deferred until the HTTP API slice requires it.

deferrals.title: Out-of-process workflow execution
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Out-of-process workflow execution is deferred; initial execution remains in-process behind durable records.

deferrals.title: Graph database
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: A graph database is deferred; initial Knowledge uses relational graph structures.

deferrals.title: Search and embedding infrastructure
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Dedicated search and embedding infrastructure is deferred until demonstrated need.

deferrals.title: Production retention, encryption, backup, and deletion policy completion
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Production lifecycle policies are deferred to their approved security and operations decisions.

deferrals.title: Production environment activation
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Production profiles and activation are deferred until recompilation and production validation requirements can be satisfied.

deferrals.title: Production-grade secret resolver
deferrals.normalized_publication_statement: Production secret-provider integration is deferred; environment-variable and mock resolvers are implemented first.
